title: image-processor pods OOMKilled, intermittent 502s
error_signature: Pod OOMKilled (exit code 137); intermittent 502 during restarts
summary: image-processor pods have been OOMKilled and restarted roughly every 20 minutes since ~03:00 UTC, causing intermittent 502s as in-flight requests are dropped. Memory climbs steadily between restarts (sawtooth) and resets after each kill.

ground_truth.root_cause: image-processor has a memory leak: decoded image buffers are stored in a module-level cache that is never evicted, so RSS grows monotonically until the container hits its 2GB memory limit and the kernel OOMKills it (exit 137). Each kill drops in-flight requests (502) and the cycle repeats. The 502s are a symptom of the restart loop, not a request-handling bug.
key_evidence: log: 'Container OOMKilled (exit code 137), restarting pod image-processor-7c9' recurring every ~20m
log: heap climbs to '1980MB/2048MB' before each kill and resets to ~210MB after restart (sawtooth)
502s cluster exactly at restart timestamps; the unbounded 'image buffer cache' has no eviction configured
expected_fix_themes: fix the leak: bound/evict the image buffer cache (LRU with a max size) or stop caching decoded buffers
raise the memory limit only as a stopgap and add a heap/RSS alert that fires before OOM
add graceful shutdown + readiness gating so restarts drain in-flight requests instead of returning 502
